data.title: Ynvisible announces non-brokered private placement
data.description:
• Ynvisible Interactive (TSXV:YNV:CA) on Friday said it has arranged a non-brokered private placement financing of up to 5M common shares at a price of $0.10 per share, for aggregate gross proceeds of up to $500,000.
  • Proceeds from the private placement will be used as working capital and for general corporate purposes.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Ryan Kalt acquires 1M common shares of Dixie Gold
data.description:
• Dixie Gold (TSXV:DG:CA) said on Friday that Ryan Kalt has acquired 1M common share units of the company on a direct basis through a private placement.
  • Acquired Units were acquired by Kalt on a direct basis for investment purposes, with cash consideration of $50,000 paid to the Issuer.
  • Kalt now owns and controls, on a direct and indirect basis a total of 11.7M common shares of Dixie Gold, representing approximately 36.81% of the Issuer’s currently issued and outstanding common shares.

data.title: Google wins delay of order to open up app store from Epic case
data.description:
Google (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) has won a temporary delay of a directive to open up its app store while a federal court rules on the company's appeal.

U.S. District Judge James Donato allowed for the delay of his order for Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) to allow for developers to set up their own app marketplaces independent of its Play Store, and to do so by Nov. 1 -- a deadline Google bristled at, suggesting it wasn't technically possible.

The Oct. 7 order stemmed from Google's loss in a lawsuit from Epic Games, developer of hit videogame Fortnite, after years of challenges from Epic to app-store leaders Google and Apple (AAPL).

The Ninth Circuit Court of Appeals is set to look at the handling of the trial, which Google lost last December.

Epic Games is majority owned by its CEO Tim Sweeney, though Tencent (OTCPK:TCEHY) maintains a 35% stake and Sony (SONY) a 5.4% holding.

See what's at stake in the recent ruling over allowing alternative app stores.

data.title: Euda Health files for $100M mixed securities shelf
data.description:
• Euda Health (NASDAQ:EUDA) filed a prospectus related to the offer and sale of $100M mixed securities shelf.
  • This preliminary prospectus is not an offer to sell these securities.
  • Filing

data.title: Public Storage appoints Chris Sambar as COO
data.description:
• Public Storage (NYSE:PSA) on Friday has appointed Chris Sambar as chief operating officer.
  • Most recently, Sambar was president at AT&T Communications. He also served in various leadership positions, including sales, operations, and corporate strategy, since joining AT&T in 2002.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Ontrak files offering of common stock
data.description:
• Ontrak (NASDAQ:OTRK) filed preliminary prospectus for offering up to 6,449,481 shares of our common stock issuable upon the exercise of outstanding warrants.
  • SEC Filing

data.title: Flux Power receives non-compliance letter from Nasdaq
data.description:
• Flux Power (NASDAQ:FLUX) said on Friday that it has received a letter from Nasdaq, notifying the Company that it was not in compliance with requirements of Nasdaq’s listing rules as a result of not having timely filed its Annual Report with the Securities and Exchange Commission.
  • The company said it is working to complete its Form 10-K and plans to file its Form 10-K as promptly as practicable to regain compliance with the Listing Rule.

data.title: S&P, Dow both notch new record closes as Wall Street posts six-week win streak
data.description:
Wall Street on Friday gained on the back of a post-earnings surge in streaming giant Netflix (NFLX), which in turn helped the overall benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500) to its longest weekly win streak of 2024.

The S&P (SP500) ended +0.40% at 5,864.67 points, while the blue-chip Dow (DJI) eked out a climb of +0.09% to conclude at 43,275.91 points. Both gauges notched new record closes. The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) settled +0.63% at 18,489.55 points.

All 11 S&P sectors closed in the green except Energy.

For the week, the S&P (SP500) improved +0.85%, helped by solid bank earnings and economic data that reinforced soft landing hopes. The Nasdaq (COMP:IND) added +0.80%, while the Dow (DJI) added +0.96%.

"The unyielding bull market continues to unnerve anyone over-thinking matters. Election risk? Market up. VIX elevated? Market up. Major semiconductor sector company releases numbers a day early and tells investors it’s tough out there? Market up. Apocalyptic weather up and down the Eastern seaboard? Market up," Alex King, investing group leader of Cestrian Capital Research, told Seeking Alpha.

"When markets are like this it is best, in our view, to just be carried on upwards by the wave. Be ready to hedge or take profits, sure, but don’t blink too soon. We continue to look upwards into year-end," King added.

On Friday, Netflix (NFLX) swelled +11.1%, as analysts and investors cheered the streaming leader's quarterly report that easily topped expectations for profit and subscriber growth. Netflix (NFLX) added 5.07M global paid net members in Q3 to take its total membership to 282.72M.

Apple (AAPL) ended +1.2%, after Counterpoint Research data showed that the tech behemoth's iPhone 16 sales rose 20% in China since their debut. Wedbush Securities suggested that the development showed a "renaissance of growth" had begun for Apple (AAPL).

Meanwhile, CVS Health (CVS) shed -5.2% and ended among the top percentage losers on the S&P 500 (SP500). The Woonsocket, R.I.-based company operates pharmacies, CVS Caremark - one of the largest U.S. pharmacy benefits managers - and also offers health insurance.

Amid activist pressure, CVS (CVS) announced the replacement of its top boss Karen Lynch with company veteran and Caremark president David Joyner. It also withdrew its previously provided 2024 guidance.

American Express (AXP) ticked down -3.1% and ended as another top S&P percentage loser, after the credit card issuer slightly trimmed its full-year revenue growth guidance.

Friday's economic calendar was fairly light. Data from the U.S. Census Bureau showed housing starts and building permits unexpectedly slumped in September.

"Housing starts & permits were softer, but the main story is that housing starts continue to run below the level of completions, which means units under construction will continue to decline, weighing on residential investment," Renaissance Macro Research said on X.

Turning to the fixed-income markets, U.S. Treasury yields were lower. The benchmark 10-year yield (US10Y) was down 2 basis points to 4.08%, while the shorter-end 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 3 basis points to 3.96%.

For more, see how Treasury yields have done across the curve on the Seeking Alpha bond page.

  • UBS upgrades view on U.S. stocks to attractive - here are its top picks
  • Oppenheimer buy-rated ideas: BSX, MDT, and one hold: CYTK

data.title: Calibre Mining cuts full-year production guidance after Q3 miss
data.description:
Calibre Mining (OTCQX:CXBMF) -4.2% in Friday's trading after lowering full-year production guidance following disappointing Q3 results.

Calibre (OTCQX:CXBMF) said it expects consolidated Q3 gold production of 70K-80K oz, lower than forecast due mostly to higher than anticipated historical artisanal mining activity at the Volcan open pit, where full-year production is expected to come in ~20K oz below budget because of the high historical artisanal mining activity.

The miner said it now forecasts full-year output of 230K-240K oz, vs. previous expectations of 275K-300K oz, with 235K-255K oz coming from Nicaragua and 40K-45K oz from Nevada.

Calibre's (OTCQX:CXBMF) production miss likely was a one-time event but still surprising, TD Cowen analyst Steven Green says, noting Q3 sales of 46K gold oz were well below his outlook for 70K oz, which is a break for a company that has been a "very consistent and predictable producer in Nicaragua since taking over the operations.

Green says Calibre (OTCQX:CXBMF) underestimated the actual level of artisanal mining that had occurred in the Volcan mine, which resulted in lower than expected ore mined, and he is "comfortable" it will not be repeated.

data.title: Dana exploring sale of off-highway unit - Bloomberg
data.description:
Automotive supplier Dana Inc. (NYSE:DAN) is conducting a sale of its off-highway unit. Dana ticked higher by 0.7%.

The company is working with advisers on a potential sale of the business, according to traders, who cited a Bloomberg report on Friday, which cited people familiar with the matter. The unit may be worth a few billion dollars in a sale, and the sale process is at a very early stage.

The news comes after a Betaville alert on Monday speculated that the automotive supplier may have attracted takeover interest.

Dana (DAN) is scheduled to report Q3 results on Oct. 30.
